Path of Exile 2 pricing assistant that uses local machine-learning models, hotkey overlays, Discord-based lookups, and stash scraping to help players price rare items faster. It is built for quick decisions, practical comparisons, and less time spent manually filtering trade listings.

pages.title: StashSage for Path of Exile 2 (POE2) | Item Price Predictions & Trade Tools
pages.meta_description: StashSage for Path of Exile 2 (POE2) helps you price items faster with prediction overlays, Discord API pricing, stash scraping, and prediction history tools.
pages.text:
Top 10 Mods for Item Value
Browse trained models (collapsed by default)
Category Segment Model
Importances are normalized per model. Values shown as percentages.
#
Feature
Importance
Why StashSage
Does this sound like you?
You pick up items that could be good, but aren't quite sure
Your stash fills up with "I'll price this later" items
Pricing through the in-game trade UI feels tedious, so you avoid it
Typing mods, comparing rolls, and overthinking prices takes more effort than it's worth
You don't need a perfect price - you just want a smart starting point
One hotkey, instant comparable items, list it, and get back to mapping
No Single Ground Truth
There is no single "right" answer for an item's price. Demand changes with every league and patch,
so yesterday's completed sales may not represent today's market.
Trading Reality
The data we see is from items that haven't yet sold. Expensive listings linger while good deals disappear,
so raw trade results are noisy and unreliable on their own. If anything, they may represent a price ceiling.
How We Measure Value
StashSage focuses on practical shortcuts instead of perfect accuracy. We look for tools that help you:
Judge a reasonable listing price in seconds.
Track and encode market dynamics.
Avoid obvious price trolls or stale posts.
Bottom line: our goal is faster choices. If you spend less time scrolling trade listings and
more time playing, the system is doing its job, even if some items never sell.
Explore Features
Discord API Prediction UI Prediction History Stash Scrape
Discord API
Select or hover over the item in game for which you'd like to see price predictions.
Press CTRL+C to copy the item's in-game description.
Paste the description to StashSage Serve bot in our Discord channel to receive your predictions.
Note: some features such as mod value filtering and visualizations not available on Discord API
Prediction UI
Select or hover over the item in game for which you'd like to see price predictions.
Press CTRL+1 (or custom hotkey) to bring up the (unfiltered) prediction overlay.
Press CTRL+2 (or custom hotkey) to bring up the mod filters and filtered prediction overlay.
Review the predictions (#1 = xgboost, #2 = knn) and compare to similar items.
Prediction History
Press CTRL + 3 (or custom hotkey) to bring up the prediction history overlay.
Review snapshots of previous item predictions, including the item descriptions.
Compare to selling history in your Merchant tabs to assess quality of predictions.
Using Stash Scrape
Select Stash Scrape from the main menu.
Enter your Trade API username and a target directory in which to save output files.
Enter Seconds Between Searches ; we suggest at least 300 s to minimize rate limiting.
Select the listing type (All Listings = public premium stash tabs + merchant tabs).
Select the categories to scrape; each necessitates a separate search as in step 3.
Upon pipeline completion, fetch the output file from the target directory.
Discussion
Models are trained on current, unsold listings , so the data can be noisy. Even reasonably priced items may not sell.
Models #1 ( XGBoost ) and #2 ( KNN ) use different approaches, so their predictions may sometimes disagree. Both provide useful perspectives on an item's potential value.
The Model #1 visualization shows how an item's predicted value compares with other items in the same category.
XGBoost is a tree-based model that evaluates the value of each modifier and how modifiers interact with one another. It uses patterns from all items in the dataset—their modifiers and listed prices—to estimate how the target item's complete set of stats may affect its value.
KNN uses XGBoost's modifier weighting to identify the items in the dataset most similar to the target item. These “nearest” items do not need to match perfectly; they can still provide useful comparisons even when some modifiers differ. The mean and median prices of these comparable items are then used to estimate the target item's value.
Careful when using Model #2 (knn) to price items with less than 6-mods, comparing to 6-m items can be tricky. Ask yourself, is a 4-m item more similar to (a) a 4-m item with the same 4-m but very different values, or (b) a 5-m item with 3 of the exact same mods and values, and 2 non-overlapping mods?
Models trained to price rare items, not magic (forthcoming) or unique .
Overlay is optimized for Windowed Fullscreen only.
New models trained every few days and posted for download.
Notes
The model ignores "mark of the abyssal lord", "allocates passive", "on corruption" mods.
The model ignores socketables as they are not considered an underlying modifier.
Items with quality and socketables have had their modifier values normalized to their base values.
Armour, evasion, energy shield mods are represented in the base stat values.
Legal
Terms of Service
By downloading or using StashSage, you agree to these terms. This software is provided
"as is," without warranties of any kind. You are responsible for how you use the app and for
compliance with the terms of any third-party services you access. We may update or discontinue
features at any time. To the maximum extent permitted by law, we are not liable for indirect,
incidental, or consequential damages arising from use of the app.
Contact: datawiseguysllc@gmail.com
Privacy Policy
StashSage is designed for local operation. We do not collect, store, or sell personal
data. Any data processed by the app (e.g., configuration, model inputs/outputs) stays on
your device. If the app calls third-party APIs you authorize, the data is transmitted only as
required to perform the requested operation and is not retained by us. Crash logs or analytics are
not sent to our servers.
If this policy changes in the future, we will update this page and the app's release notes.
For questions or requests, email datawiseguysllc@gmail.com .
Quick links: Terms of Service - Privacy Policy
Explore DataWiseGuys
Interested in learning more about other DataWiseGuys projects? Check them out below.
SocialSync - social media orchestration and automation with optional AI for small businesses.
Want to brainstorm developing your own app with analytics or machine learning? Reach out to us .

You pick up items that could be good, but aren't quite sure Your stash fills up with "I'll price this later" items Pricing through the in-game trade UI feels tedious, so you avoid it Typing mods, comparing rolls, and overthinking prices takes more effort than it's worth You don't need a perfect price - you just want a smart starting point One hotkey, instant comparable items, list it, and get back to mapping No Single Ground Truth There is no single "right" answer for an item's price. Demand changes with every league and patch, so yesterday's completed sales may not represent today's market. Trading Reality The data we see is from items that haven't yet sold. Expensive listings linger while good deals disappear, so raw trade results are noisy and unreliable on their own. If anything, they may represent a price ceiling. How We Measure Value StashSage focuses on practical shortcuts instead of perfect accuracy. We look for tools that help you: Judge a reasonable listing price in seconds. Track and encode market dynamics. Avoid obvious price trolls or stale posts. Bottom line: our goal is faster choices. If you spend less time scrolling trade listings and more time playing, the system is doing its job, even if some items never sell. Explore Features Discord API Prediction UI Prediction History Stash Scrape Discord API Select or hover over the item in game for which you'd like to see price predictions. Press CTRL+C to copy the item's in-game description. Paste the description to StashSage Serve bot in our Discord channel to receive your predictions. Note: some features such as mod value filtering and visualizations not available on Discord API Prediction UI Select or hover over the item in game for which you'd like to see price predictions. Press CTRL+1 (or custom hotkey) to bring up the (unfiltered) prediction overlay. Press CTRL+2 (or custom hotkey) to bring up the mod filters and filtered prediction overlay. Review the predictions (#1 = xgboost, #2 = knn) and compare to similar items. Prediction History Press CTRL + 3 (or custom hotkey) to bring up the prediction history overlay. Review snapshots of previous item predictions, including the item descriptions. Compare to selling history in your Merchant tabs to assess quality of predictions. Using Stash Scrape Select Stash Scrape from the main menu. Enter your Trade API username and a target directory in which to save output files. Enter Seconds Between Searches ; we suggest at least 300 s to minimize rate limiting. Select the listing type (All Listings = public premium stash tabs + merchant tabs). Select the categories to scrape; each necessitates a separate search as in step 3. Upon pipeline completion, fetch the output file from the target directory. Discussion Models are trained on current, unsold listings , so the data can be noisy. Even reasonably priced items may not sell. Models #1 ( XGBoost ) and #2 ( KNN ) use different approaches, so their predictions may sometimes disagree. Both provide useful perspectives on an item's potential value. The Model #1 visualization shows how an item's predicted value compares with other items in the same category. XGBoost is a tree-based model that evaluates the value of each modifier and how modifiers interact with one another. It uses patterns from all items in the dataset—their modifiers and listed prices—to estimate how the target item's complete set of stats may affect its value. KNN uses XGBoost's modifier weighting to identify the items in the dataset most similar to the target item. These “nearest” items do not need to match perfectly; they can still provide useful comparisons even when some modifiers differ. The mean and median prices of these comparable items are then used to estimate the target item's value. Careful when using Model #2 (knn) to price items with less than 6-mods, comparing to 6-m items can be tricky. Ask yourself, is a 4-m item more similar to (a) a 4-m item with the same 4-m but very different values, or (b) a 5-m item with 3 of the exact same mods and values, and 2 non-overlapping mods? Models trained to price rare items, not magic (forthcoming) or unique . Overlay is optimized for Windowed Fullscreen only. New models trained every few days and posted for download. Notes The model ignores "mark of the abyssal lord", "allocates passive", "on corruption" mods. The model ignores socketables as they are not considered an underlying modifier. Items with quality and socketables have had their modifier values normalized to their base values. Armour, evasion, energy shield mods are represented in the base stat values. Legal Terms of Service By downloading or using StashSage, you agree to these terms. This software is provided "as is," without warranties of any kind. You are responsible for how you use the app and for compliance with the terms of any third-party services you access. We may update or discontinue features at any time. To the maximum extent permitted by law, we are not liable for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages arising from use of the app. Contact: datawiseguysllc@gmail.com Privacy Policy StashSage is designed for local operation. We do not collect, store, or sell personal data. Any data processed by the app (e.g., configuration, model inputs/outputs) stays on your device. If the app calls third-party APIs you authorize, the data is transmitted only as required to perform the requested operation and is not retained by us. Crash logs or analytics are not sent to our servers. If this policy changes in the future, we will update this page and the app's release notes. For questions or requests, email datawiseguysllc@gmail.com . Quick links: Terms of Service - Privacy Policy Explore DataWiseGuys Interested in learning more about other DataWiseGuys projects? Check them out below. SocialSync - social media orchestration and automation with optional AI for small businesses. Want to brainstorm developing your own app with analytics or machine learning? Reach out to us .

pages.title: SocialSync
pages.meta_description: Automated content generation and posting across social media.
headings: Automate your company's content generation and posting across social media.
pages.text:
Manual Images + Posts
Bring your own assets and captions when you already have everything prepared.
Manual Images + AI Posts
Add image descriptions to help AI write posts. Optionally, let SocialSync select images randomly from a pool of candidates to rotate content.
AI Images + AI Posts
Generate images from instructions or juxtaposed options (e.g., "the dog wears a hat/tie with an ocean/mountain
in the background"), then let AI craft the post using the selected image context.
Video Forthcoming
Video integration is forthcoming using Sora and other AI tools.

pages.text:
SocialSync
Interested in Beta Testing? Reach out at socialsyncdwg@gmail.com or join our Discord to connect.
Automate your content generation and posting across social media.
SocialSync makes it simple to post content to Facebook (Groups), Instagram, Twitter/X, and TikTok on a preset schedule. Content can be manually generated or produced by AI models (OpenAI, Google, Anthropic, xAI), with workflows that pass media or text from previous steps to keep context flowing.
The best part? It's easy to set up and designed to save time and reduce costs.

WATCH THE DEMO!
SocialSync Demo -- Automate Your Social Media Posting
Overview 1. Credentials 2. Profiles 3. Media 4. Instructions 5. Posts 6. Scheduling 7. Analytics 8. Backup
Overview
One workspace for creating, scheduling, and improving social posts
SocialSync brings your accounts, content ideas, media, schedules, results, and backups into one organized workflow. Set it up once, then reuse what works instead of rebuilding every post from scratch.
Connect the social accounts you want SocialSync to publish to.
Save reusable rules for captions, images, videos, hashtags, links, tone, and review steps.
Keep separate brands, campaigns, or clients organized in separate profiles.
Schedule posts, review what happened, and improve future content from the results.
Export your setup so important work is easy to preserve, move, or restore.
Image instruction example
Instructions keep context flowing through every step of the pipeline.
Text instruction example
Pair media and text instructions so context flows in either direction.
Core UI
Simple, organized UI where credentials, media, and instructions are easy to manage by profile.
1. Social Media Credentials and Authorization
Connect the accounts SocialSync will use
Add the login, authorization, and notification details SocialSync needs before it can post or report on your behalf.
Save the account access details for Facebook Groups, Instagram, Twitter/X, and TikTok.
Update passwords, API keys, or authorization details when a connected account changes.
Choose the email address that should receive success, failure, and follow-up notifications.
Notification email
Show the email address SocialSync uses for success and failure notifications.
Social media logins
Show the saved platform logins that the workflow can use.
API keys & account password
Show the API key and password controls used to update account access.
2. Profiles and Social Account Linking
Keep each brand or campaign organized
Profiles let you separate accounts, media, instructions, schedules, and posting behavior so content goes to the right audience every time.
Create a profile for each brand, client, campaign, or content style you want to manage separately.
Link each profile to the social accounts that should receive its posts.
Keep each profile's assets, instructions, schedules, and publishing settings from mixing with another.
Manage Profiles & Preferences
Show how one profile stays separated from another with its own settings and posting setup.
Set Each Profile's Social Connections
Show the social accounts linked to this profile so you can confirm the right publishing connections.
Set (Auto) Platform Deployment
Show whether this profile publishes automatically or waits for review before it goes live.
3. Media Libraries and Access
Build a reusable library of images and videos
Upload the media you use often, organize it into folders, and choose which profiles are allowed to use each asset.
Keep approved images and videos in one place instead of hunting through local folders.
Limit media access by profile when brands, clients, or campaigns need to stay separate.
Use library assets directly in posts or as reference material for AI-assisted visuals.
Image Store setup
Use Image Store to verify the library is populated and organized before building instructions and posts around it.
Video Store setup
Video assets can be reviewed and organized the same way so the media side of the workflow is ready before post construction begins.
Linked profile confirmation
Linked-profile controls are where shared media gets narrowed down for the profiles that should actually be allowed to use it.
4. Instructions and Prompting
Save the rules for what SocialSync should create
Instructions tell SocialSync how captions, hashtags, links, images, and videos should look and sound, whether content is manual, AI-assisted, or mixed.
Text Instructions define caption style, hashtags, links, tone, and required callouts.
Media Instructions define whether images or videos are chosen manually, selected at random, or generated with AI.
Variety settings rotate approved styles, backgrounds, themes, or angles so posts do not feel repetitive.
Add dates, reference images, or other context when a post needs extra direction.
Text instruction example
Show the text instruction panel with captions, links, hashtags, and required callouts.
Media instruction example
Show the image or video instruction that selects or generates media for the example.
Add Variety configuration
Show the Add Variety box or related dynamic fields that keep the example fresh.
5. Posts and Variety Weights
Turn ideas into reusable post templates
Post templates combine text, media, and generation rules so you can repeat successful formats without writing every post from scratch.
Start with copy and let SocialSync select or generate matching media.
Start with an image, video, or prompt and let SocialSync write matching caption copy.
Use weights to control how often each approved template appears in a random schedule.
Post template overview
Show the reusable post template that will be published or drafted.
Text and media flow setting
Show whether the post is configured as text-to-media or media-to-text.
Weighting configuration
Show the weight values that determine how strongly the post is favored during random selection.
6. Scheduling and Publishing
Publish consistently without babysitting every post
Choose when profiles should post, whether SocialSync should pick from a set of approved templates, and whether posts publish automatically or wait for review.
Set recurring posting times for each profile.
Choose one approved post or let SocialSync rotate through a weighted template pool.
Publish automatically when you want hands-off posting, or hold drafts for review when approval matters.
Use notification emails and Usage history to confirm what ran and investigate anything that failed.
Scheduler configuration
Show the saved schedule for the example profile, including the selected post or random mode.
Draft or publishing outcome
Show the review state, draft destination, or final publish confirmation.
Usage confirmation
Show the Usage entry that confirms the scheduled run executed correctly.
7. Usage, Analytics, and Traffic
See what ran and what worked
Review completed runs, live links, costs, platform analytics, and website traffic so you can adjust content based on results instead of guessing.
Usage shows what ran, whether it succeeded, and where the live post or draft can be found.
Analytics surfaces impressions and performance data across connected platforms.
Traffic tracking shows whether social posts are sending people to your website.
Use results to improve prompts, media choices, profile settings, and schedules over time.
Usage reference view
Show the Usage page entry that records the completed run and keeps links available.
Analytics dashboard
Show the analytics view used to review impressions or profile performance after publishing.
Traffic or downstream measurement
Show the traffic or reporting view used to measure website impact.
8. Backup & Restore
Protect your setup and restore it when needed
Export your instructions as a backup, move a known-good setup to another profile, or restore work after a major change.
Export creates a CSV backup of Image, Video, and Text instructions.
Back up one profile, several profiles, or the full instruction set.
Use Merge to add missing instructions without duplicating rows that already exist.
Use Replace when the imported setup should become the new source of truth for a profile.
Review the preview before restoring so missing media references are easy to catch.
Create a backup before major restructures and after important setup milestones.
Export page overview
Show the Export page with profile selection and CSV export options before downloading a backup.
Import upload and mode
Show the Import page where a CSV backup is uploaded and the restore mode is chosen.
Import preview and confirmation
Show the import preview or confirmation screen so it is clear which instructions will be merged or replaced.
Quick links: Terms of Service · Privacy Policy · Data Deletion

Use weights to control how often each approved template appears in a random schedule. Post template overview Show the reusable post template that will be published or drafted. Text and media flow setting Show whether the post is configured as text-to-media or media-to-text. Weighting configuration Show the weight values that determine how strongly the post is favored during random selection. 6. Scheduling and Publishing Publish consistently without babysitting every post Choose when profiles should post, whether SocialSync should pick from a set of approved templates, and whether posts publish automatically or wait for review. Set recurring posting times for each profile. Choose one approved post or let SocialSync rotate through a weighted template pool. Publish automatically when you want hands-off posting, or hold drafts for review when approval matters. Use notification emails and Usage history to confirm what ran and investigate anything that failed. Scheduler configuration Show the saved schedule for the example profile, including the selected post or random mode. Draft or publishing outcome Show the review state, draft destination, or final publish confirmation. Usage confirmation Show the Usage entry that confirms the scheduled run executed correctly. 7. Usage, Analytics, and Traffic See what ran and what worked Review completed runs, live links, costs, platform analytics, and website traffic so you can adjust content based on results instead of guessing. Usage shows what ran, whether it succeeded, and where the live post or draft can be found. Analytics surfaces impressions and performance data across connected platforms. Traffic tracking shows whether social posts are sending people to your website. Use results to improve prompts, media choices, profile settings, and schedules over time. Usage reference view Show the Usage page entry that records the completed run and keeps links available. Analytics dashboard Show the analytics view used to review impressions or profile performance after publishing. Traffic or downstream measurement Show the traffic or reporting view used to measure website impact. 8. Backup & Restore Protect your setup and restore it when needed Export your instructions as a backup, move a known-good setup to another profile, or restore work after a major change. Export creates a CSV backup of Image, Video, and Text instructions. Back up one profile, several profiles, or the full instruction set. Use Merge to add missing instructions without duplicating rows that already exist. Use Replace when the imported setup should become the new source of truth for a profile. Review the preview before restoring so missing media references are easy to catch. Create a backup before major restructures and after important setup milestones. Export page overview Show the Export page with profile selection and CSV export options before downloading a backup. Import upload and mode Show the Import page where a CSV backup is uploaded and the restore mode is chosen. Import preview and confirmation Show the import preview or confirmation screen so it is clear which instructions will be merged or replaced. Quick links: Terms of Service · Privacy Policy · Data Deletion

pages.text:
Terms of Service
Last updated: 2026-07-09T12:49:20-05:00
These Terms govern your access to and use of SocialSync, a workflow platform operated by Data Wiseguys, LLC for social-media content management, scheduling, analytics, traffic tracking, and connected account automation.
1. Acceptance of these Terms
By accessing or using SocialSync, you agree to be bound by these Terms and by our Privacy Policy . If you do not agree, do not use the service.
2. What SocialSync provides
SocialSync is designed to help account owners and authorized team members manage content workflows and connected distribution channels. Depending on enabled features, SocialSync may allow you to:
store and organize images, videos, prompts, templates, and scheduling data;
connect third-party services such as TikTok, X, Meta properties, YouTube, and AI providers;
generate draft content, queue scheduled runs, review logs, and track analytics or site traffic; and
publish or prepare content through provider integrations when you explicitly authorize those actions.
3. Eligibility and account responsibility
You may use SocialSync only if you are legally able to enter into a binding agreement and are authorized to act for the account, business, or organization whose profiles you connect.
You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your login credentials.
You are responsible for all actions taken through your account, team members, and connected provider accounts.
You must provide accurate information when creating accounts, configuring profiles, and connecting integrations.
4. Your content and your instructions
You retain responsibility for the content, media, prompts, settings, schedules, and account instructions you upload or configure in SocialSync.
You represent that you have the rights needed to upload, schedule, generate, publish, or otherwise use that content.
You grant SocialSync permission to store, process, transform, and transmit that content only as needed to operate the service on your behalf.
You remain responsible for reviewing generated outputs, final posts, and provider-specific publishing settings before they are used.
5. Connected platforms and provider access
Some SocialSync features depend on third-party services and APIs. If you connect TikTok or another provider, you authorize SocialSync to use the scopes, tokens, account identifiers, and related integration data needed to perform the features you enable. When supported, deleting a stored credential from SocialSync may also trigger best-effort revocation of the associated provider token or permission.
Provider functionality may change, break, be rate-limited, or become unavailable without notice.
You must comply with the terms, policies, and community or developer rules of each connected provider.
You may revoke provider access at any time from the provider's account settings or from SocialSync where supported.
SocialSync does not control third-party platform review outcomes, API availability, or moderation decisions.
6. Prohibited uses
You may not use SocialSync to violate law, third-party rights, or connected platform policies. Prohibited uses include, without limitation:
attempting unauthorized access to systems, accounts, or APIs;
submitting unlawful, deceptive, infringing, abusive, or malicious content;
circumventing rate limits, approval gates, access controls, or security controls; and
using SocialSync in a way that could harm the service, other users, or third-party providers.
7. Service changes, availability, and beta features
SocialSync may add, remove, suspend, or modify features at any time. Some features may be marked as beta, pre-release, or experimental and may be less stable than general-availability features.
We do not guarantee uninterrupted availability, error-free operation, or compatibility with every provider workflow.
Maintenance, outages, provider-side changes, and operational issues may affect service behavior.
8. Suspension or termination
We may suspend, restrict, or terminate access if we reasonably believe your use violates these Terms, creates legal or security risk, harms the platform, or misuses connected providers.
You may stop using SocialSync at any time. You may also permanently delete your account from the Credentials page in the app, and data deletion requests are handled as described on the Data Deletion page .
9. Disclaimers
SocialSync is provided on an "as is" and "as available" basis. To the maximum extent permitted by law, Data Wiseguys, LLC disclaims warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, non-infringement, availability, accuracy, and uninterrupted operation.
10. Limitation of liability
To the maximum extent permitted by law, Data Wiseguys, LLC will not be liable for indirect, incidental, special, consequential, exemplary, or punitive damages, or for loss of profits, revenues, data, business opportunities, goodwill, or anticipated savings arising from or related to SocialSync or connected provider services.
11. Changes to these Terms
We may revise these Terms from time to time by posting updated terms at this URL. Your continued use of SocialSync after updated Terms are posted constitutes acceptance of the revised Terms.
12. Contact
Data Wiseguys, LLC
Email: socialsyncdwg@gmail.com

pages.meta_description: SocialSync Privacy Policy for account data, connected platforms, analytics, and OAuth-based integrations including TikTok.
pages.text:
Privacy Policy
Last updated: 2026-07-09T12:49:20-05:00
This Privacy Policy explains how Data Wiseguys, LLC ("SocialSync", "we", "our") collects, uses, stores, and discloses information when you use SocialSync, including when you connect third-party services such as TikTok through OAuth or API access and when you delete accounts or credentials from the app.
1. Scope of this Policy
This Policy applies to information processed through SocialSync websites, login surfaces, application pages, workflow tools, analytics features, traffic tracking features, and connected platform integrations.
2. Categories of information we collect
Depending on how you use SocialSync, we may collect and process the following categories of information:
account data such as usernames, profile names, permissions, login-related settings, and account status;
workflow data such as prompts, templates, schedules, publishing settings, run logs, analytics settings, and profile configuration;
media and metadata such as uploaded file names, descriptions, usage notes, storage paths, and media associations;
integration data such as OAuth tokens, refresh tokens, account identifiers, authorization status, selected scopes, and provider metadata needed to operate enabled integrations;
usage and operational data such as job outcomes, timestamps, error messages, and feature usage records; and
website or traffic data submitted through the SocialSync traffic tracker, such as site identifiers, page paths, timestamps, and request metadata needed to measure traffic.
3. How we collect information
directly from you when you create accounts, upload media, configure profiles, or submit settings;
from connected platforms when you authorize SocialSync to access account-scoped features through OAuth or API scopes;
from automated workflow execution when runs, schedules, analytics jobs, and publishing actions operate on your behalf; and
from normal product operation, troubleshooting, security review, and usage logging.
4. How we use information
We use information to operate, maintain, and improve SocialSync. Examples include:
authenticating users and managing account access;
storing and executing your requested workflow configuration;
publishing, drafting, or scheduling content when you authorize those actions;
providing analytics, reporting, cost visibility, traffic reporting, and run-history features;
maintaining service reliability, abuse prevention, and incident response; and
responding to support requests, compliance requests, deletion requests, and credential-revocation requests.
5. Connected platforms, including TikTok
If you connect TikTok or another provider, SocialSync processes only the integration data reasonably required to perform the actions and scopes you enable.
For TikTok, this may include account identifiers, OAuth tokens, token expiry metadata, authorization status, draft or posting metadata, and other account-scoped data returned by approved scopes.
For other providers such as X, Meta, YouTube, OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, or xAI, SocialSync may exchange the minimum data necessary to support the specific feature you requested.
When you delete an individual stored credential from SocialSync, we may attempt best-effort revocation of the related provider token or permission where the provider supports it.
Each provider processes data under its own policies, terms, and developer requirements.
6. When we may disclose information
We do not sell your personal data. We may disclose information in the following limited situations:
to connected platforms and service providers when required to perform a feature you requested;
to infrastructure, hosting, storage, analytics, or security vendors that help us operate SocialSync;
to comply with law, legal process, or valid governmental requests; or
to protect the rights, safety, integrity, and security of SocialSync, our users, or the public.
7. Data retention
We retain information for as long as reasonably necessary to provide the service, preserve security, maintain records of workflow activity, satisfy contractual commitments, and comply with legal obligations.
Workflow configuration, logs, and integration metadata may remain available until removed, overwritten, or deleted.
When you delete your account from the Credentials page or submit a valid deletion request, we will delete or de-identify account-scoped data except where retention is required for security, fraud prevention, or legal compliance.
8. Security
We use reasonable administrative, technical, and operational safeguards intended to protect stored information. No internet or software system can be guaranteed perfectly secure, so you should also protect your own credentials and connected accounts.
9. Your choices and rights
You may request access, correction, or deletion of account-scoped data by contacting us.
You may permanently delete your account from within SocialSync on the Credentials page.
You may disconnect provider accounts from within SocialSync where supported.
You may delete individual stored credentials from the Credentials page, and where supported SocialSync will attempt best-effort revocation of the related provider token or permission.
You may revoke provider permissions directly from the provider's account settings.
You may request deletion by following the process on our Data Deletion page .
10. Children's privacy
SocialSync is not directed to children and is not intended for use by individuals who are not legally permitted to use the connected services and agreements involved. Do not use SocialSync if you are not legally authorized to do so.
11. Changes to this Policy
We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time by posting a revised version at this URL. Continued use of SocialSync after an update is posted means the updated Policy will apply going forward.
12. Contact
Data Wiseguys, LLC
Email: socialsyncdwg@gmail.com

No internet or software system can be guaranteed perfectly secure, so you should also protect your own credentials and connected accounts. 9. Your choices and rights You may request access, correction, or deletion of account-scoped data by contacting us. You may permanently delete your account from within SocialSync on the Credentials page. You may disconnect provider accounts from within SocialSync where supported. You may delete individual stored credentials from the Credentials page, and where supported SocialSync will attempt best-effort revocation of the related provider token or permission. You may revoke provider permissions directly from the provider's account settings. You may request deletion by following the process on our Data Deletion page . 10. Children's privacy SocialSync is not directed to children and is not intended for use by individuals who are not legally permitted to use the connected services and agreements involved. Do not use SocialSync if you are not legally authorized to do so. 11. Changes to this Policy We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time by posting a revised version at this URL. Continued use of SocialSync after an update is posted means the updated Policy will apply going forward. 12. Contact Data Wiseguys, LLC Email: socialsyncdwg@gmail.com

pages.text:
Ledger Lite
Developed by DataWiseguys, LLC.
Download APK
Email us or join our Discord !
Write privately.
Summarize instantly.
Ask freely.
Ledger Lite is a free Android app for writing and cleaning notes, generating summaries, and asking questions about your own data using an on-device LLM.
Your data remains private and is never shared.
What to expect on first launch: the app opens to a setup screen and downloads its local AI models into app-private storage. Leave it open on Wi-Fi;
the summary model is large and dominates the first-run download.
Screenshots
Add a setup screenshot at
docs/screenshots/setup.jpg
Setup
The app checks and downloads the summary and embedding models automatically. This is where the user should expect the large initial download.
Shows per-model install state
Shows download progress and byte counts
Allows retry if the network drops
Add a Notes screenshot at
docs/screenshots/notes.jpg
Notes
The Notes screen separates the currently active note from older records so you can pull a previous record back into focus quickly.
Active section for the selected note
Records list for prior notes
Fast jump from historical records back into the active view
Toggle between Created (your notes) and Generated (AI summaries)
Add a Compose screenshot at
docs/screenshots/compose.jpg
Compose
Capture a note with a title, editable date, body text, and reusable tags. The date defaults to today but can be changed for backfilled notes.
Manual date field in YYYY-MM-DD
Up to five reusable tags
One note can carry multiple tags
AI-powered Clean up rewrites your entry for clarity and formatting
Add an Ask screenshot at
docs/screenshots/ask.jpg
Ask
Your question goes to the local LLM, which decides whether to answer via semantic search (finding a relevant subset of documents) or brute-force (reading all your documents). You can help by supplying a date range and tags to reduce the document pool it has to read over.
LLM chooses semantic or brute-force approach per question
Date range and tags reduce the brute-force document set
Answer synthesized from the retrieved context
Add a Summarize screenshot at
docs/screenshots/summarize.jpg
Summarize
Summarize generates daily/weekly/monthly/yearly documents and can run automatically on a configurable daily schedule to keep summaries current.
Update — skips already-summarized documents, adds new ones
Rebuild — starts from scratch or from a specified date
Set a daily runtime to run summarization automatically
Export or import summary data to back up or move to another device
Remote AI
Connect over Tailscale
Ledger Lite can reach an Ollama instance running on another machine — a laptop, desktop, or home server —
over a private Tailscale network. Your traffic stays end-to-end encrypted and never touches a public server.
1. Install Tailscale on both devices
Install Tailscale on the machine running Ollama and on your Android phone. Sign in to the same account on both — they will
appear on the same tailnet automatically with stable private IPs that don't change between sessions.
2. Bind Ollama to accept remote connections
By default Ollama only listens on 127.0.0.1:11434 and won't accept
connections from your phone. Set the OLLAMA_HOST environment variable
before starting the server to open it on all interfaces:
macOS / Linux
OLLAMA_HOST=0.0.0.0:11434 ollama serve
Windows (PowerShell)
$env:OLLAMA_HOST="0.0.0.0:11434"; ollama serve
Alternatively, set OLLAMA_HOST to your machine's specific
Tailscale IP (e.g. 100.x.y.z:11434 ) to restrict listening to
the Tailscale interface only — the more locked-down option.
3. Find your machine's Tailscale IP
On the host machine, run tailscale ip -4 to get its IPv4 address
on the tailnet (typically starts with 100. ). You can also find it
in the Tailscale admin console or the system tray app.
4. Enter the address in Ledger Lite
Open the app, go to Settings → AI Connection , and set the Ollama host to
your machine's Tailscale IP and port — for example http://100.x.y.z:11434 .
Use the Test connection button to verify the app can reach the server before
running a request.
Add a settings screenshot at
docs/screenshots/ollama_settings.jpg
Settings → AI Connection
Ollama connection
Point the app at your Tailscale IP and port, then run a test to confirm the connection
before making inference requests.
Enter the full http:// URL with port
Test connection button confirms reachability
Works over Wi-Fi, LTE, or any network Tailscale supports
Keep it locked down: binding Ollama to 0.0.0.0 opens the port
on all network interfaces, including Wi-Fi. If you only want the Tailscale interface exposed, bind to your
Tailscale IP directly and confirm your host firewall blocks port 11434 on other
interfaces. Tailscale encrypts all traffic between devices on your tailnet, but the Ollama port itself has
no authentication — anyone on your local network could reach it if the firewall is open.

Monitor local LLM endpoint health, schedule Automations around usage resets, fork thread context to another agent, and drive threads by voice with Vibe mode.

pages.text:
RolyCode
One Tool to Rule Them All: RolyCode for AI Coding Agents
Live Free, Vibe Hard
RolyCode is a local web control surface for the coding-agent CLIs you already use, such as Codex, Claude Code, Gemini, Grok, Cursor, and local model chat. It runs the backend on your machine and gives you one desktop/mobile interface for starting threads, sending prompts, reviewing output, and driving repo workflows.
It uses local workflow-model calls, such as Ollama, llama.cpp, LM Studio, or another local OpenAI-compatible server, to refine prompts, generate thread titles and commit messages, and parse commands from private Telegram and Discord bots.
Use the Android companion app on Android, or use Telegram and Discord bots from iPhone or Android. The desktop server can stay local, run on your LAN, or be reached over Tailscale when you configure a private auth token.
Developed by DataWiseguys, LLC .
Join our Discord or email us to ask questions or give feedback.
W Windows EXE Stable release desktop build L Linux binary Stable release x64 build A Android APK Optional phone companion
Videos Screenshots Automations Vibe mode Setup
Videos
Demos of RolyCode
Android App
Replicate Your Desktop on RolyCode Mobile App
Use the Android mobile companion app to replicate your desktop's interface on the go.
Telegram
Connect Your Private Telegram Bot using Botfather
Talk to your private bots using a short list of commands to streamline your vibe coding experience.
Discord
Connect Your Private Discord Bot using Developer Portal
Talk to your private bots using a short list of commands to streamline your vibe coding experience.
Screenshots
Thread UI
Designed to one-shot issues per thread; simplicity is king
Use one thread to manage an entire workflow or update, start to finish.
MULTI-AGENT
Control your coding-agent CLIs from one app
Supports coding-agent CLIs such as Codex, Claude Code, Gemini, Grok, and Cursor, plus local model chat through engines such as Ollama, llama.cpp, LM Studio, and local OpenAI-compatible servers.
Prompting
Send controls for direct, refined, or planned prompts
Push raw text to an agent CLI when you know exactly what you want, or route it through the local workflow model when it needs refinement or planning.
Git workflow
Built-in Pull and PR controls
Keep the repo workflow inside the thread: sync to main , make changes, commit, push, and create GitHub pull requests.
Desktop + mobile clients
Desktop and Android clients both share same backend
Use the larger desktop surface at your desk, then switch between devices and pick up where you left off using mobile while relaxing on your couch.
Telegram & Discord bots
Use the Android companion app, or Telegram and Discord from iPhone
Android gets the native companion app. iPhone and Android users can also send private bot commands such as /new , /pull , and /pr through Telegram or Discord.
Repo setup
Browse to any project or folder on desktop to add files
Use the desktop file picker to add a repository, then name it with an alias so it is easy to find in the project list.
Image chat
Include images in chat
Paste screenshots straight with Ctrl+V or the + button when the conversation needs visual context.
Track usage
Watch Claude, Codex, and Gemini usage from one place
See live or estimated token and budget usage in one panel so you can keep an eye on resets and current burn across coding agents. 🔥😈
Local models
Watch your local LLM endpoints stay healthy
Keep an eye on every local inference endpoint from one place, so you know a model is reachable before you route a prompt to it.
Endpoint status
See health and status of every local endpoint
RolyCode probes each configured local endpoint, such as Ollama, llama.cpp, LM Studio, or another OpenAI-compatible server, and shows whether it is connected or unavailable, the endpoint host, the active model, when it was last checked, and any connection error.
Automations
Run tasks on a schedule and beat the usage clock
Schedule recurring or one-time thread runs so RolyCode keeps working while you are away, then use the timing to squeeze more out of every usage reset.
Run tasks on a schedule
Queue recurring or one-time thread runs so routine work kicks off on its own, without you sitting at the keyboard.
Continue when usage resets
Set an automation to continue a thread the moment your usage window resets, so long jobs pick right back up.
Start early for an extra reset
Kick off usage early in the morning to bank an extra reset for the day and get more total headroom.
Fork
Fork a thread's context into another thread or agent
Fork
Pass existing context to a new thread and agent
Fork this thread's context into a new editable thread, then hand it to another agent: route simple tasks straight through, or fork big tasks after planning so the follow-up agent starts with the full plan.
Vibe mode
Talk to RolyCode with your voice
Start fresh
Start a new thread by voice
Open Vibe mode to start a new thread and interact with RolyCode directly through voice chat.
Pick up a thread
Resume a thread by voice
Start Vibe on any existing thread to pick up where you left off with hands-free voice chat.
Setup
Install the desktop app first, then connect mobile if you want it.
The desktop package is the required base install because it owns the local backend, launcher restart, .rolycode.env ,
Telegram & Discord settings, auth token, local workflow-model configuration, and the coding-agent CLIs on your machine. Use the Windows EXE on Windows or
the Linux binary on Linux. The Android APK is optional and connects back to the desktop server.
Desktop app (required)
Download and run the Windows .exe or the Linux binary on the machine that already has your console agent CLIs installed. Install a local workflow-model engine too, such as Ollama, llama.cpp, LM Studio, or another local OpenAI-compatible server. For Windows, Cursor Agent requires a supported Linux environment via WSL/Ubuntu.
Start each desired CLI manually once in each repo directory so the CLI can sign in, accept trust prompts, and give trust to that folder. This only needs to be done once per repo.
Leave Agent CLI mode set to Local YOLO in desktop Settings for the simplest setup with git shortcuts and direct file edits, then enable the agents you want to appear in new-thread dropdowns.
Important: Local YOLO grants full local permissions to launched agent CLIs.
If you want a clean setup, move the .exe into a new folder, create .rolycode.env in the same folder, and define keys and values before launching: ROLYCODE_DEVICE_NAME=PC
AUTH_TOKEN=replace-with-long-private-token
ROLYCODE_AGENT_CLI_SANDBOX=off
ROLYCODE_ENABLED_AGENTS=codex,claude-code,gemini,ollama,grok,cursor
ROLYCODE_WORKFLOW_INFERENCE_ENGINE=ollama
ROLYCODE_WORKFLOW_OLLAMA_MODELS=gemma4:e2b,gemma4:e4b
ROLYCODE_WORKFLOW_OLLAMA_MODEL=gemma4:e4b
ROLYCODE_WORKFLOW_CONTEXT_WINDOW=4096
ROLYCODE_WORKFLOW_TIMEOUT_SECONDS=120
ROLYCODE_CODING_INFERENCE_ENGINE=ollama
ROLYCODE_CODING_OLLAMA_MODELS=laguna-xs.2:q4_K_M,gemma4:26b
ROLYCODE_CODING_OLLAMA_MODEL=laguna-xs.2:q4_K_M
ROLYCODE_CODING_CONTEXT_WINDOW=8192
ROLYCODE_CODING_TIMEOUT_SECONDS=120
# Use either the Ollama block or the llama.cpp block for each model role.
# Alternative: llama.cpp / GGUF files
# ROLYCODE_WORKFLOW_INFERENCE_ENGINE=llamacpp
# ROLYCODE_WORKFLOW_LLAMACPP_GGUF_FILES=C:\models\workflow-small.gguf,C:\models\workflow-large.gguf
# ROLYCODE_WORKFLOW_LLAMACPP_GGUF_FILE=C:\models\workflow-small.gguf
# ROLYCODE_CODING_INFERENCE_ENGINE=llamacpp
# ROLYCODE_CODING_LLAMACPP_GGUF_FILES=C:\models\coder-small.gguf,C:\models\coder-large.gguf
# ROLYCODE_CODING_LLAMACPP_GGUF_FILE=C:\models\coder-large.gguf
# Alternative: LM Studio model names
# ROLYCODE_WORKFLOW_INFERENCE_ENGINE=lmstudio
# ROLYCODE_WORKFLOW_LMSTUDIO_MODEL=qwen2.5-coder
# ROLYCODE_CODING_INFERENCE_ENGINE=lmstudio
# ROLYCODE_CODING_LMSTUDIO_MODEL=qwen2.5-coder
# Alternative: another local OpenAI-compatible server
# ROLYCODE_WORKFLOW_INFERENCE_ENGINE=openai-compatible
# ROLYCODE_WORKFLOW_OPENAI_COMPATIBLE_MODEL=local-workflow
# ROLYCODE_CODING_INFERENCE_ENGINE=openai-compatible
# ROLYCODE_CODING_OPENAI_COMPATIBLE_MODEL=local-coder
DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN=
DISCORD_USER_ID=
ROLYCODE_DISCORD_LISTENER_ENABLED=false
TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=
ROLYCODE_TELEGRAM_LISTENER_ENABLED=false
The downloadable template contains every environment field persisted by desktop Settings, with safe defaults and commented examples for Ollama, llama.cpp, LM Studio, and other local OpenAI-compatible servers.
Download the .rolycode.env-example template , copy it beside the executable, and rename it to .rolycode.env before launching. It contains placeholders only; add your own private tokens locally.
That same .rolycode.env can be used for reinstalls or moved between machines so you do not have to rebuild new client setups from scratch.
Multiple desktop instances can run on different devices, each adapting to its own device name, so the mobile apps can target a specific machine or switch between them if you hit rate limits or use different agent credentials.
Android app (optional)
Download the APK if you want the native companion app on Android. iPhone users can use the Telegram or Discord bot path instead.
Optional remote access over Tailscale: install Tailscale on the desktop and mobile device, sign both into the same tailnet, and set a long private mobile auth token in RolyCode desktop Settings.
In desktop Settings → Remote access , click Set up Tailscale access and accept the one Windows approval prompt. RolyCode configures persistent Tailscale HTTPS automatically; no Administrator terminal or firewall command is required.
When setup succeeds, enter the displayed Tailscale URL and your mobile app auth token in the companion settings. Settings shows Repair Tailscale access automatically if the saved connection becomes stale.
Mobile Settings needs the desktop device name, server URL, and access token. Runtime configuration stays on desktop because the device owns the backend, .rolycode.env , launcher restart, Discord, tokens, and local workflow models.
Telegram & Discord
Telegram and Discord bot integration stays configured on the desktop, and the private bots can send commands from your phone to the desktop server.
Add bot tokens in desktop Settings. Turn on Run Telegram listener or Run Discord listener . Listener toggles control whether the desktop actively receives bot messages.
Pairing is separate from listening. Open the pairing window for 5 or 15 minutes while you send /pair from Telegram or Discord, then approve the desktop popup.
Use Always open only during setup or testing, then use Close now after pairing. Existing approved chats continue working after the pairing window closes.
Remote access
Remote access over Tailscale, made simple
Reach your desktop backend from anywhere without hand-editing firewall rules or running Administrator terminals. Simplicity is king.
One-click Tailscale setup
In desktop Settings → Remote access , click Set up Tailscale access and accept the one Windows approval prompt.
RolyCode provisions persistent Tailscale HTTPS for you, so no Administrator terminal or firewall command is required.
Settings shows Repair Tailscale access automatically if the saved connection becomes stale.
Same backend, anywhere
Install Tailscale on the desktop and mobile device, sign both into the same tailnet, and set a long private auth token.
Enter the displayed Tailscale URL and token in the companion app. The desktop server can stay local, run on your LAN, or be reached over Tailscale, same backend either way.
Discord
Optional Discord bot setup.
Discord Bot Setup Guide (.docx)
Discord bot setup
Create a new application in the Discord Developer Portal .
Open the Bot tab and enable Message Content Intent .
Invite the bot to a private server that you control and keep it available.
Send the bot a direct message once so it can message you back.
Copy the bot token and paste into desktop’s Discord Bot Token setting.
Enable Developer Mode in Discord, right-click your user, copy your user ID, and paste into desktop's Discord User ID setting.
Turn on Run Discord listener , open the Discord pairing window for 5 or 15 minutes, then send /pair to the bot.
Approve the desktop popup, click Save (Restart Required) , restart RolyCode, and close the pairing window after approval.
Note: the video and document were originally created for a different app, StashSage, but same steps apply to RolyCode.
Legal
Terms of Service
Usage terms for the software, your responsibilities, acceptable use boundaries, and liability limitations.
Open dedicated page Privacy Notice
How the app handles local data, optional third-party integrations, and what remains under your control.
Open dedicated page AI Output Disclaimer
What generated output can and cannot be trusted for, and where human review is still required.
Open dedicated page
pages.document_text:
RolyCode
RolyCode is a local desktop and mobile control surface for coding-agent CLIs and local workflow models: Codex, Claude Code, Gemini, Grok, Cursor, Ollama, llama.cpp, LM Studio, and local OpenAI-compatible servers. Monitor local LLM endpoint health, schedule Automations around usage resets, fork thread context to another agent, and drive threads by voice with Vibe mode.
RolyCode
Demos of RolyCode
Replicate Your Desktop on RolyCode Mobile App
Connect Your Private Telegram Bot using Botfather
Connect Your Private Discord Bot using Developer Portal
Designed to one-shot issues per thread; simplicity is king
Control your coding-agent CLIs from one app
Send controls for direct, refined, or planned prompts
Built-in Pull and PR controls
Desktop and Android clients both share same backend
Use the Android companion app, or Telegram and Discord from iPhone
Browse to any project or folder on desktop to add files
Include images in chat
Watch Claude, Codex, and Gemini usage from one place
Watch your local LLM endpoints stay healthy
See health and status of every local endpoint
Run tasks on a schedule and beat the usage clock
Fork a thread's context into another thread or agent
Pass existing context to a new thread and agent
Talk to RolyCode with your voice
Start a new thread by voice
Resume a thread by voice
Install the desktop app first, then connect mobile if you want it.
Remote access over Tailscale, made simple
Optional Discord bot setup.
RolyCode
One Tool to Rule Them All: RolyCode for AI Coding Agents
Live Free, Vibe Hard
RolyCode is a local web control surface for the coding-agent CLIs you already use, such as Codex, Claude Code, Gemini, Grok, Cursor, and local model chat. It runs the backend on your machine and gives you one desktop/mobile interface for starting threads, sending prompts, reviewing output, and driving repo workflows.
It uses local workflow-model calls, such as Ollama, llama.cpp, LM Studio, or another local OpenAI-compatible server, to refine prompts, generate thread titles and commit messages, and parse commands from private Telegram and Discord bots.
Use the Android companion app on Android, or use Telegram and Discord bots from iPhone or Android. The desktop server can stay local, run on your LAN, or be reached over Tailscale when you configure a private auth token.
Developed by DataWiseguys, LLC .
Join our Discord or email us to ask questions or give feedback.
W Windows EXE Stable release desktop build L Linux binary Stable release x64 build A Android APK Optional phone companion
Videos Screenshots Automations Vibe mode Setup
Videos
Demos of RolyCode
Android App
Replicate Your Desktop on RolyCode Mobile App
Use the Android mobile companion app to replicate your desktop's interface on the go.
Telegram
Connect Your Private Telegram Bot using Botfather
Talk to your private bots using a short list of commands to streamline your vibe coding experience.
Discord
Connect Your Private Discord Bot using Developer Portal
Talk to your private bots using a short list of commands to streamline your vibe coding experience.
Screenshots
Thread UI
Designed to one-shot issues per thread; simplicity is king
Use one thread to manage an entire workflow or update, start to finish.
MULTI-AGENT
Control your coding-agent CLIs from one app
Supports coding-agent CLIs such as Codex, Claude Code, Gemini, Grok, and Cursor, plus local model chat through engines such as Ollama, llama.cpp, LM Studio, and local OpenAI-compatible servers.
Prompting
Send controls for direct, refined, or planned prompts
Push raw text to an agent CLI when you know exactly what you want, or route it through the local workflow model when it needs refinement or planning.
Git workflow
Built-in Pull and PR controls
Keep the repo workflow inside the thread: sync to main , make changes, commit, push, and create GitHub pull requests.
Desktop + mobile clients
Desktop and Android clients both share same backend
Use the larger desktop surface at your desk, then switch between devices and pick up where you left off using mobile while relaxing on your couch.
Telegram & Discord bots
Use the Android companion app, or Telegram and Discord from iPhone
Android gets the native companion app. iPhone and Android users can also send private bot commands such as /new , /pull , and /pr through Telegram or Discord.
Repo setup
Browse to any project or folder on desktop to add files
Use the desktop file picker to add a repository, then name it with an alias so it is easy to find in the project list.
Image chat
Include images in chat
Paste screenshots straight with Ctrl+V or the + button when the conversation needs visual context.
Track usage
Watch Claude, Codex, and Gemini usage from one place
See live or estimated token and budget usage in one panel so you can keep an eye on resets and current burn across coding agents. 🔥😈
Local models
Watch your local LLM endpoints stay healthy
Keep an eye on every local inference endpoint from one place, so you know a model is reachable before you route a prompt to it.
Endpoint status
See health and status of every local endpoint
RolyCode probes each configured local endpoint, such as Ollama, llama.cpp, LM Studio, or another OpenAI-compatible server, and shows whether it is connected or unavailable, the endpoint host, the active model, when it was last checked, and any connection error.
Automations
Run tasks on a schedule and beat the usage clock
Schedule recurring or one-time thread runs so RolyCode keeps working while you are away, then use the timing to squeeze more out of every usage reset.
Run tasks on a schedule
Queue recurring or one-time thread runs so routine work kicks off on its own, without you sitting at the keyboard.
Continue when usage resets
Set an automation to continue a thread the moment your usage window resets, so long jobs pick right back up.
Start early for an extra reset
Kick off usage early in the morning to bank an extra reset for the day and get more total headroom.
Fork
Fork a thread's context into another thread or agent
Fork
Pass existing context to a new thread and agent
Fork this thread's context into a new editable thread, then hand it to another agent: route simple tasks straight through, or fork big tasks after planning so the follow-up agent starts with the full plan.
Vibe mode
Talk to RolyCode with your voice
Start fresh
Start a new thread by voice
Open Vibe mode to start a new thread and interact with RolyCode directly through voice chat.
Pick up a thread
Resume a thread by voice
Start Vibe on any existing thread to pick up where you left off with hands-free voice chat.
Setup
Install the desktop app first, then connect mobile if you want it.
The desktop package is the required base install because it owns the local backend, launcher restart, .rolycode.env ,
Telegram & Discord settings, auth token, local workflow-model configuration, and the coding-agent CLIs on your machine. Use the Windows EXE on Windows or
the Linux binary on Linux. The Android APK is optional and connects back to the desktop server.
Desktop app (required)
Download and run the Windows .exe or the Linux binary on the machine that already has your console agent CLIs installed. Install a local workflow-model engine too, such as Ollama, llama.cpp, LM Studio, or another local OpenAI-compatible server. For Windows, Cursor Agent requires a supported Linux environment via WSL/Ubuntu.
Start each desired CLI manually once in each repo directory so the CLI can sign in, accept trust prompts, and give trust to that folder. This only needs to be done once per repo.
Leave Agent CLI mode set to Local YOLO in desktop Settings for the simplest setup with git shortcuts and direct file edits, then enable the agents you want to appear in new-thread dropdowns.
Important: Local YOLO grants full local permissions to launched agent CLIs.
If you want a clean setup, move the .exe into a new folder, create .rolycode.env in the same folder, and define keys and values before launching: ROLYCODE_DEVICE_NAME=PC
AUTH_TOKEN=replace-with-long-private-token
ROLYCODE_AGENT_CLI_SANDBOX=off
ROLYCODE_ENABLED_AGENTS=codex,claude-code,gemini,ollama,grok,cursor
ROLYCODE_WORKFLOW_INFERENCE_ENGINE=ollama
ROLYCODE_WORKFLOW_OLLAMA_MODELS=gemma4:e2b,gemma4:e4b
ROLYCODE_WORKFLOW_OLLAMA_MODEL=gemma4:e4b
ROLYCODE_WORKFLOW_CONTEXT_WINDOW=4096
ROLYCODE_WORKFLOW_TIMEOUT_SECONDS=120
ROLYCODE_CODING_INFERENCE_ENGINE=ollama
ROLYCODE_CODING_OLLAMA_MODELS=laguna-xs.2:q4_K_M,gemma4:26b
ROLYCODE_CODING_OLLAMA_MODEL=laguna-xs.2:q4_K_M
ROLYCODE_CODING_CONTEXT_WINDOW=8192
ROLYCODE_CODING_TIMEOUT_SECONDS=120
# Use either the Ollama block or the llama.cpp block for each model role.
# Alternative: llama.cpp / GGUF files
# ROLYCODE_WORKFLOW_INFERENCE_ENGINE=llamacpp
# ROLYCODE_WORKFLOW_LLAMACPP_GGUF_FILES=C:\models\workflow-small.gguf,C:\models\workflow-large.gguf
# ROLYCODE_WORKFLOW_LLAMACPP_GGUF_FILE=C:\models\workflow-small.gguf
# ROLYCODE_CODING_INFERENCE_ENGINE=llamacpp
# ROLYCODE_CODING_LLAMACPP_GGUF_FILES=C:\models\coder-small.gguf,C:\models\coder-large.gguf
# ROLYCODE_CODING_LLAMACPP_GGUF_FILE=C:\models\coder-large.gguf
# Alternative: LM Studio model names
# ROLYCODE_WORKFLOW_INFERENCE_ENGINE=lmstudio
# ROLYCODE_WORKFLOW_LMSTUDIO_MODEL=qwen2.5-coder
# ROLYCODE_CODING_INFERENCE_ENGINE=lmstudio
# ROLYCODE_CODING_LMSTUDIO_MODEL=qwen2.5-coder
# Alternative: another local OpenAI-compatible server
# ROLYCODE_WORKFLOW_INFERENCE_ENGINE=openai-compatible
# ROLYCODE_WORKFLOW_OPENAI_COMPATIBLE_MODEL=local-workflow
# ROLYCODE_CODING_INFERENCE_ENGINE=openai-compatible
# ROLYCODE_CODING_OPENAI_COMPATIBLE_MODEL=local-coder
DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN=
DISCORD_USER_ID=
ROLYCODE_DISCORD_LISTENER_ENABLED=false
TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=
ROLYCODE_TELEGRAM_LISTENER_ENABLED=false
The downloadable template contains every environment field persisted by desktop Settings, with safe defaults and commented examples for Ollama, llama.cpp, LM Studio, and other local OpenAI-compatible servers.
Download the .rolycode.env-example template , copy it beside the executable, and rename it to .rolycode.env before launching. It contains placeholders only; add your own private tokens locally.
That same .rolycode.env can be used for reinstalls or moved between machines so you do not have to rebuild new client setups from scratch.
Multiple desktop instances can run on different devices, each adapting to its own device name, so the mobile apps can target a specific machine or switch between them if you hit rate limits or use different agent credentials.
Android app (optional)
Download the APK if you want the native companion app on Android. iPhone users can use the Telegram or Discord bot path instead.
Optional remote access over Tailscale: install Tailscale on the desktop and mobile device, sign both into the same tailnet, and set a long private mobile auth token in RolyCode desktop Settings.
In desktop Settings → Remote access , click Set up Tailscale access and accept the one Windows approval prompt. RolyCode configures persistent Tailscale HTTPS automatically; no Administrator terminal or firewall command is required.
When setup succeeds, enter the displayed Tailscale URL and your mobile app auth token in the companion settings. Settings shows Repair Tailscale access automatically if the saved connection becomes stale.
Mobile Settings needs the desktop device name, server URL, and access token. Runtime configuration stays on desktop because the device owns the backend, .rolycode.env , launcher restart, Discord, tokens, and local workflow models.
Telegram & Discord
Telegram and Discord bot integration stays configured on the desktop, and the private bots can send commands from your phone to the desktop server.
Add bot tokens in desktop Settings. Turn on Run Telegram listener or Run Discord listener . Listener toggles control whether the desktop actively receives bot messages.
Pairing is separate from listening. Open the pairing window for 5 or 15 minutes while you send /pair from Telegram or Discord, then approve the desktop popup.
Use Always open only during setup or testing, then use Close now after pairing. Existing approved chats continue working after the pairing window closes.
Remote access
Remote access over Tailscale, made simple
Reach your desktop backend from anywhere without hand-editing firewall rules or running Administrator terminals. Simplicity is king.
One-click Tailscale setup
In desktop Settings → Remote access , click Set up Tailscale access and accept the one Windows approval prompt.
RolyCode provisions persistent Tailscale HTTPS for you, so no Administrator terminal or firewall command is required.
Settings shows Repair Tailscale access automatically if the saved connection becomes stale.
Same backend, anywhere
Install Tailscale on the desktop and mobile device, sign both into the same tailnet, and set a long private auth token.
Enter the displayed Tailscale URL and token in the companion app. The desktop server can stay local, run on your LAN, or be reached over Tailscale, same backend either way.
Discord
Optional Discord bot setup.
Discord Bot Setup Guide (.docx)
Discord bot setup
Create a new application in the Discord Developer Portal .
Open the Bot tab and enable Message Content Intent .
Invite the bot to a private server that you control and keep it available.
Send the bot a direct message once so it can message you back.
Copy the bot token and paste into desktop’s Discord Bot Token setting.
Enable Developer Mode in Discord, right-click your user, copy your user ID, and paste into desktop's Discord User ID setting.
Turn on Run Discord listener , open the Discord pairing window for 5 or 15 minutes, then send /pair to the bot.
Approve the desktop popup, click Save (Restart Required) , restart RolyCode, and close the pairing window after approval.
Note: the video and document were originally created for a different app, StashSage, but same steps apply to RolyCode.
Legal
Terms of Service
Usage terms for the software, your responsibilities, acceptable use boundaries, and liability limitations.
Open dedicated page Privacy Notice
How the app handles local data, optional third-party integrations, and what remains under your control.
Open dedicated page AI Output Disclaimer
What generated output can and cannot be trusted for, and where human review is still required.
Open dedicated page

# Data WiseGuys, LLC

The available input contains only a validation-error excerpt from a prior response, not the underlying website evidence bundle. It is therefore not possible to produce a grounded company profile or verify the referenced people, services, events, or pricing details.

The website lists Roland Heinze, Dylan Cael as team members.

Released products include SocialSync, Ledger Lite, StashSage, RolyCode.

Products in development include ProspectRecon, Insight Connect.

# Data WiseGuys, LLC

_RolyCode RolyCode is a local desktop and mobile control surface for coding-agent CLIs and local workflow models: Codex, Claude Code, Gemini, Grok, Cursor, Ollama, llama.cpp, LM Studio, and local OpenAI-compatible servers._

## Executive Summary

The available input contains only a validation-error excerpt from a prior response, not the underlying website evidence bundle. It is therefore not possible to produce a grounded company profile or verify the referenced people, services, events, or pricing details.

The website lists Roland Heinze, Dylan Cael as team members.

Released products include SocialSync, Ledger Lite, StashSage, RolyCode.

Products in development include ProspectRecon, Insight Connect.

## Products

### Released

- [SocialSync](https://www.socialsyncdwg.com) — Social media operations workspace for building and running profile-based posting workflows across Facebook, Instagram, X, and TikTok. It supports manual or AI-generated media and copy, reusable instruction stores, scheduled jobs, and analytics follow-up.
- [Ledger Lite](https://www.ledgerlitedwg.com) — Local-first Android journal for quick note capture, incremental rollups, and semantic search. It keeps notes on-device, builds daily through yearly rollups from the last dirty checkpoint, and falls back to built-in local heuristics when imported models are not present.
- [StashSage](https://rheinze08.github.io/StashSage/) — Path of Exile 2 pricing assistant that uses local machine-learning models, hotkey overlays, Discord-based lookups, and stash scraping to help players price rare items faster. It is built for quick decisions, practical comparisons, and less time spent manually filtering trade listings.
- [RolyCode](https://www.rolycode.com) — Local-first command center for coding-agent workflows, prompts, and multi-turn threads. RolyCode combines a local web UI, prompt compilation with Ollama, image attachments, desktop-to-mobile access, and routing into configured Codex, Claude Code, Gemini, Grok, or Ollama sessions.

### In Development

- ProspectRecon — Prospect intelligence pipeline that turns raw company lists into ranked, explainable target accounts. It crawls websites, stores reusable page corpora, refines ICP concepts with AI, and scores domains against the latest prospect criteria.
- Insight Connect — AI-assisted analytics workspace for turning domain data into dashboards, generated code, and business answers. It combines modular pipelines, notebook and file context, dataset copilots, and domain modules such as supply chain and charity analysis.

## Leadership & Team

- Roland Heinze — Founder, CEO
- Dylan Cael — Key Contributor

## Services & Offerings

- Workflow configuration, logs, and integration metadata may remain available until removed, overwritten, or deleted.
- For TikTok, this may include account identifiers, OAuth tokens, token expiry metadata, authorization status, draft or posting metadata, and other account-scoped data returned by approved scopes.
- Multiple desktop instances can run on different devices, each adapting to its own device name, so the mobile apps can target a specific machine or switch between them if you hit rate limits or use different agent credentials.

## Markets & Use Cases

Primary customers: Path of Exile players, municipal, construction, events, residential.

- Ledger Lite Write privately.
- Your data remains private and is never shared.
- It contains placeholders only;.
- Invite the bot to a private server that you control and keep it available.
- SocialSync - social media orchestration and automation with optional AI for small businesses.
- Connected platforms and provider access Some SocialSync features depend on third-party services and APIs.
- SocialSync does not control third-party platform review outcomes, API availability, or moderation decisions.
- Prohibited uses You may not use SocialSync to violate law, third-party rights, or connected platform policies.

## Differentiators & Value Proposition

- Select the listing type (All Listings = public premium stash tabs + merchant tabs)
- Expensive listings linger while good deals disappear, so raw trade results are noisy and unreliable on their own.
- Ledger Lite Ledger Lite is a free Android app for writing and cleaning notes, generating summaries, and asking questions about your own data using an on-device LLM.

## Process & Operations

- Remote access over Tailscale, made simple Optional Discord bot setup.
- Screenshots Setup Notes Compose Ask Summarize Connect over Tailscale 1.
- Setup Install the desktop app first, then connect mobile if you want it.
- Export your setup so important work is easy to preserve, move, or restore.
- Create a backup before major restructures and after important setup milestones.
- Use Always open only during setup or testing, then use Close now after pairing.
- Use Replace when the imported setup should become the new source of truth for a profile.
- Maintenance, outages, provider-side changes, and operational issues may affect service behavior.
- Discord Bot Setup Guide (.docx) Discord bot setup Create a new application in the Discord Developer Portal.
- When setup succeeds, enter the displayed Tailscale URL and your mobile app auth token in the companion settings.

## Safety & Compliance

- Schedule posts, review what happened, and improve future content from the results.
- Use weights to control how often each approved template appears in a random schedule.
- You are responsible for how you use the app and for compliance with the terms of any third-party services you access.
- Sign in to the same account on both — they will appear on the same tailnet automatically with stable private IPs that don't change between sessions.
- Video Store setup Video assets can be reviewed and organized the same way so the media side of the workflow is ready before post construction begins.
- Leave Agent CLI mode set to Local YOLO in desktop Settings for the simplest setup with git shortcuts and direct file edits, then enable the agents you want to appear in new-thread dropdowns.
- When you delete your account from the Credentials page or submit a valid deletion request, we will delete or de-identify account-scoped data except where retention is required for security, fraud prevention, or legal compliance.

posts.text: @Bill_Maizpants Appreciate the callout, I'm using an LLM with my other app SocialSync to do the posting, I clarified it needs to say currency not gold. Thank you.
@MadCADLad Appreciate the callout, I'm using an LLM with my other app SocialSync to do the posting, I clarified it needs to say currency not gold. Thank you.
@gayerthancock Thanks for your opinion and feedback, appreciate the discussion.
@Faith83537 Thanks for the feedback, good luck to you and happy to help provide interpretation or answer questions if you have more.

@ARozayy23 I personally didn't find much value in simple querying tools like EE and SK and wanted to try my own approach -- they seemed like too much comparison and manual work still come up with a price. I don't struggle with this tool but recognize there is a learning curve.

@tr1tech That's all fair, we don't control the market prices others set for their items. It's just a tool to help you compare to the most similar items listed; you could filter to guarantee 25 MS, but otherwise I'd argue that item is mathematically similar in terms of mods and values.
@pfaraGG Yes we are training new releases on current league data.
@tragnesie Hey there, just got back from vacation and tested the downloadable app and am not currently seeing that error. If you're still encountering it please let me know, attempted to fix.

@MDubshooter Thanks for the feedback, it's an old example from a few seasons ago. It's shown as 1800 exalts, not 3300. I would invite you to come over to the Discord channel and try out the free API to test some more current examples.

@ToRecallE @KarlJakober Let me know if you have any questions, happy to answer and provide clarity

@KarlJakober @ToRecallE Appreciate the feedback. I am pretty sure the entire app code is available open source. Please let us know what visibility can be improved, you also can just use the Discord Bot API and not need to install the app, it just offers some nice extra features
@ToRecallE @ARozayy23 All fair and Xgboost tries to reduce noise based on the average item price, similar items can be tricky though if you pull a troll offer agreed
@tragnesie Thank you I will look into this appreciate you sharing
