@b0urne_ Aye, we started exploring magic bases for amulets and rings, probably will add models for those 2 specific categories in an updated release soon. Thanks for the feedback
@LordFrz Thanks we will consider how to minimize things even more we can probably add a mini option

@trynamang @ARozayy23 The project was more of a fun research experiment that turned into something bigger, the datasets are usually something like 10,000+ items per category , sometimes smaller if the category is thinner like shield or buckler. No AI is used, only python and some ML models
@Cyberpsic0 Hey thanks for the feedback we are always working on improving the models the data is very noisy and the tool has a few nuances to understand how to use we will continue to add support

posts.text: @petllama Far from perfect but only user feedback helps us get closer to that... just like Johnathan said yesterday in that interview he did @ALegendaryDrops
@petllama Oh yeah? I haven't checked out Hermes, I use Codex, Claude Code mostly. But we started this tool long before agents were a thing about 14 months ago. How is Hermes?
@petllama We can definitely do that. Good callout, thank you. I'll add it to the dev to-do.

@renackgg Aye, I agree. They could definitely acquire StashSage and integrate it into their own app. I'm not sure how relevant "transactions" are, they might be stale or based on previous patch meta (like 0.5.0 vs 0.5.1), but def good points.

@petllama Our app produces instant predictions, shows you the most similar (mathematically) items in the dataset, and let's you more quickly reach an estimate of value. Not saying EE doesn't work, this is just a completely different approach.

@petllama Hi, Exile Exchange is fine, but it runs simple queries against existing listings, it doesn't use any ML And requires you to comb through the listings yourself; also, we use SHAP to up- or down-weight specific features like +skills to better tackle the notion of "similarity"
@g_zareba We know this is important, thank you for continuing to test it.

# Data WiseGuys, LLC — Tools Assessment

Audited website: https://rheinze08.github.io/Datawiseguysllc
Run created: 2026-07-22T13:21:10.799563+00:00
Website crawl source: source_20260722_131846_922949_ef2f02

## Executive Summary

Data WiseGuys, LLC. The assessment covers 8 website pages plus the local-presence, reputation, market, and site-health signals below.

**Top issues:** the site is not mobile-friendly.

**Not measured:** Google Reviews (GBP CID is required.); GBP Posts (GBP CID or title is required.); Review Sentiment (missing dependencies: google_reviews); Maps Competitors (GBP primary category is required.); Competitor Benchmark (disabled); AI Visibility Scan (AI visibility scan failed: timed out).

## Priority Actions

- **HIGH** — Fix the mobile experience before directing more visitors to the site. Evidence: PageSpeed classified the page as not mobile-friendly. _(Source: PageSpeed)_
- **MEDIUM** — Add a clear contact form for visitors who are not ready to call. Evidence: Website crawl did not detect a contact form. _(Source: Website Crawl)_
- **MEDIUM** — Make the primary phone number machine-readable on the site. Evidence: Website crawl did not extract a phone number from visible page text. _(Source: Website Crawl)_
- **MEDIUM** — Create or optimize service pages for proven local search demand. Evidence: Top tracked keywords total 10 monthly searches: item price prediction. _(Source: Search Demand)_
- **MEDIUM** — Verify the primary business category before relying on demand estimates. Evidence: Search demand used crawl-derived fallback context instead of a GBP primary category. _(Source: Search Demand)_
- **LOW** — Consider online booking or scheduling if appointments drive conversion. Evidence: Website crawl did not detect a booking widget. _(Source: Website Crawl)_

## Strengths

- **Strong mobile performance** — PageSpeed mobile performance score is 100/100. _(Source: PageSpeed)_
- **Valid HTTPS certificate** — SSL inspection found a valid certificate. _(Source: SSL Inspector)_

## Consultant Narrative (AI-generated)

> AI-generated interpretation; verify against the deterministic evidence below. Provider: rolycode.

# Customer Onboarding SOP

**Business version:** SOP v1 (version: 1)  
**Company:** Data WiseGuys, LLC  
**Effective from:** July 22, 2025  
**Effective through:** January 16, 2026

Purpose: Provide a consistent operating procedure for customer onboarding at Data WiseGuys, LLC, a SaaS AI startup.

1. **Assign onboarding ownership.** Leadership assigns an Operations role to coordinate the onboarding process and maintain the onboarding record.

2. **Confirm the onboarding scope.** The Operations role records the customer's requested onboarding scope, the intended use of the SaaS AI offering, and any information needed to begin the work.

3. **Prepare access and materials.** The Operations role identifies the access, information, and materials required to support onboarding and confirms that the required items are available before proceeding.

4. **Coordinate onboarding activities.** The Operations role works with the customer to complete the agreed onboarding activities and records material status updates in the onboarding record.

5. **Check readiness.** Before closing onboarding, the Operations role verifies that the required access, information, and onboarding activities have been completed or are clearly documented as outstanding.

6. **Close and hand off.** The Operations role documents the onboarding outcome, records any outstanding follow-up, and hands the customer relationship to the appropriate ongoing support or account role.

7. **Maintain the record.** The Operations role keeps the onboarding record current throughout the process and retains the completed record according to company practice.

This SOP is business version 1 and is effective during the period from July 22, 2025 through January 16, 2026.

# Customer Onboarding SOP

**Business version:** version: 2  
**Effective date:** January 16, 2026  
**Effective through:** March 29, 2026  
**Supersedes:** Customer Onboarding SOP Version 1

Purpose: Provide the operating procedure for customer onboarding and client onboarding at Data WiseGuys, LLC.

1. **Initiate customer onboarding.** Operations opens the customer onboarding process and identifies the onboarding work to be completed.

2. **Coordinate the onboarding work.** Operations coordinates the customer onboarding activities and maintains the current status of the process.

3. **Complete the onboarding process.** Operations completes the customer onboarding work and confirms that the process is ready to be closed.

4. **Close the process.** Operations closes the customer onboarding process after the required onboarding work is complete.

5. **Apply this version.** Use Customer Onboarding SOP Version 2 for the period beginning January 16, 2026, through March 29, 2026. The prior Customer Onboarding SOP Version 1 is superseded by this version.

# Customer Onboarding SOP

**version: 3**  
**Effective date:** 2026-03-29  
**Company:** Data WiseGuys, LLC

Purpose: Provide the standard procedure for client onboarding under the customer onboarding sop, version 3.

1. **Open the onboarding record.** The Customer Onboarding Team creates or locates the record for the customer and confirms that the record is associated with the correct onboarding work.

2. **Confirm the onboarding scope.** The Customer Onboarding Team reviews the information available for the onboarding and identifies any items that must be completed before work can proceed.

3. **Assign ownership.** Operations assigns an owner for the onboarding record and confirms the responsible role for each outstanding item.

4. **Complete the onboarding work.** The assigned owner coordinates the required onboarding activities, records material updates in the onboarding record, and keeps outstanding items visible until they are resolved.

5. **Review readiness.** Operations checks the onboarding record for completeness and confirms that outstanding items have been addressed or clearly documented.

6. **Close the onboarding record.** Once the readiness review is complete, the assigned owner records the completion of onboarding and closes the record.

7. **Maintain the procedure.** Leadership owns this client onboarding sop. This version: 3 procedure is effective 2026-03-29 and supersedes the prior version.

Data WiseGuys, LLC

About us

A SaaS AI startup building practical tools for data-aware work.

What we work on

Our work includes social content automation, item price prediction, local-first journaling, a Discord pricing API, stash scraping, and prediction history.

Who we serve

Our markets include Path of Exile players and municipal use cases.

Ledger Lite

Ledger Lite is a free Android app for writing and cleaning notes, generating summaries, and asking questions about your own data using an on-device LLM.

RolyCode

RolyCode is a local desktop and mobile control surface for coding-agent CLIs and local workflow models: Codex, Claude Code, Gemini, Grok, Cursor, Ollama, llama.cpp, LM Studio, and local OpenAI-compatible servers.

It supports monitoring local LLM endpoint health and scheduling Automations around usage resets.
